# Paper Outline Template

Use this template to plan a physics paper before writing.
Fill in each section with bullet points summarising what it will say,
then expand each point into prose.

!!! note "Under construction"
    A detailed guide on how to use this outline is being developed.

## Abstract

- **Setting + motivation** (1 sentence): physical system and why it matters.
- **What we do** (1–2 sentences): method, systems studied, scope — begin with "We ...".
- **Main quantitative result** (1 sentence): a specific number or comparison.
- **Broader implication** (1 sentence): how this complements existing work.

## Introduction

- **Paragraph 1 — Broad setting** (3–5 sentences): why the system is interesting from first principles.
- **Paragraph 2 — Specific opportunity** (3–5 sentences): the gap this paper fills and prior work.
- **Paragraph 3 — What this paper does** (2–4 sentences): begin with "In this work, we ...".
- **Paragraph 4 — Paper structure** (1–2 sentences, optional): section-by-section summary.

## Methods / Section II

- Key equations and assumptions.
- Diagram or sketch (becomes Figure 1).

## Results / Section III

- One subsection per key result.
- Each subsection has one central figure.

## Conclusion

- Restate the main result in one sentence.
- Broader implications.
- Outlook: two or three natural follow-up directions.

## References

- Key references identified during planning.
